events.title: Iran war live: Trump slams Iranian proposal as ceasefire hangs by a thread
events.desc: Iran's powerful parliamentary speaker says US has 'no alternative but to accept' Tehran's 14-point proposal to end war. This indicates ongoing cyber warfare and propaganda efforts by Iran to influence the peace process.

events.title: US top diplomat discusses Iran, Strait of Hormuz with UK and Australia
events.desc: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio held separate calls with his Australian and British counterparts to discuss Iran and ongoing efforts to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. This highlights ongoing diplomatic efforts to manage the fallout from the Iran conflict and maritime security concerns.

events.title: Lebanese in south refuse to flee again despite escalating Israeli strikes
events.desc: Obaida Hitto reports from southern Lebanon, where residents say they will not leave again despite intensifying strikes. This indicates continued kinetic exchanges between Israel and Hezbollah, despite previous ceasefire announcements.

events.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes killed 39
events.desc: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month. This signifies ongoing kinetic conflict along the Israel-Lebanon border.

events.title: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
events.desc: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas. This indicates a high potential for renewed kinetic conflict in the Gaza Strip.

events.title: Israeli military investigates soldier's act against Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
events.desc: The soldier was photographed putting a cigarette in the statue's mouth, in the second incident against a Christian figure in weeks. While not a direct military engagement, this incident reflects heightened tensions and potential provocations in the region.

events.title: Bowen: Strait of Hormuz standoff raises risk of sliding back into all-out war
events.desc: The US and Iran's determination to keep the pressure on each other has put the fragile Gulf ceasefire in serious jeopardy. This highlights the precarious security situation around the Strait of Hormuz and the risk of escalation.

events.title: The clandestine network smuggling Starlink tech into Iran to beat internet blackout
events.desc: Sahand tells the BBC World Service he sends satellite internet terminals into Iran to help show 'the real picture'. This indicates efforts to circumvent Iranian internet restrictions, likely involving cyber capabilities.

events.title: US issues new sanctions over Iran's oil shipments to China
events.desc: The U.S. government announced sanctions against three people and nine companies for aiding Iran's shipment of oil to China. This reflects ongoing economic pressure and diplomatic maneuvering related to the Iran conflict.

events.title: US hits China-linked Iran oil trade with sanctions ahead of Trump trip
events.desc: The sanctions come after the United States rejected Iran's latest proposal to end the war. This action signals continued kinetic pressure and economic warfare against Iran.

events.title: US issues alert to banks on IRGC efforts to evade sanctions
events.desc: The United States issued an alert to financial institutions warning of efforts by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps' to evade U.S. sanctions. This suggests ongoing efforts by Iran to use financial networks, which can have cyber implications for detection and prevention.

events.title: Turkey's border reopening unlocks new sphere of influence in Syria
events.desc: Turkey reopened the Akcakale-Tell Abyad crossing on Monday, ending an 11-year closure along the Syria border. This geopolitical development could lead to shifts in regional control and potential kinetic impacts.

events.title: Israel approves law on public trials, death penalty for October 7 detainees
events.desc: Rights groups warn that the bill makes the death penalty easier to impose and strips fair trial protections. This law could lead to increased kinetic actions and severe legal consequences for individuals involved in the October 7 attacks.

events.title: New Israeli law sets military tribunal for Hamas October 7 militants
events.desc: Israel's parliament passed a law establishing a military tribunal to try hundreds of Palestinian militants who took part in the October 7, 2023 attack on Israel. This legal development is a direct consequence of kinetic actions and may lead to further conflict.

events.title: Practice Makes Deterrence: India’s Next Nuclear Challenge at Sea
events.desc: India's submarine program and advancements in missile technology indicate a growing nuclear capability, posing a new challenge in the region and potentially altering the nuclear deterrence landscape.
